cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Andrew could have gone to the clothing store. So, the task is to determine the time window when Andrew was both free (not occupied with other activities) and the store was open. First, I need to list all the times Andrew was doing things as observed by others. Let's go through each person: 1. Andrew woke up at 5am. So he wasn't doing anything before that, but that's just the wake-up time, not an activity. 2. William saw Andrew playing tennis from 5am to 6am. So from 5 to 6, Andrew was at the tennis court. 3. Jessica saw him at the deli from 6am to 9am. That's a long time—buying lunch, so he was there from 6 to 9. 4. Tiffany saw him near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 9am to 11am. So 9 to 11, he was taking photos there. 5. John saw him at the train station from 11am to 5pm. That's a big chunk of time—11 a.m. to 5 p.m. waiting for a train. 6. Sarah saw him reading at the library from 6pm to 9pm. So evening until 9, he was in the library. 7. The clothing store was closed after 9pm. So the store is open until 9, but after that it's closed. Now, the problem is to find out between which times Andrew could have gone to the clothing store. The clothing store is open until 9pm, so any time up to 9pm is possible if he's free. We need to find any gaps in Andrew's activities where he wasn't busy. Let's outline his timeline: - 5am: Wakes up - 5am to 6am: Tennis - 6am to 9am: Deli - 9am to 11am: Leaning Tower - 11am to 5pm: Train station - 6pm to 9pm: Library - After 9pm: Store closed Looking at this, there's a big gap between 5am (wake up) and 5pm (at train station). Wait, but he was busy from 5am to 6am (tennis), then 6-9 (deli), then 9-11 (tower), then 11-5 (train). So from 5am until 5pm, he's occupied except maybe between 11am
